entries.name: BF
entries.definition: Blast furnace

entries.name: BOF
entries.definition: Basic oxygen furnace

entries.name: DRI
entries.definition: Direct reduced iron

entries.name: EAF
entries.definition: Electric arc furnace

entries.name: HBI
entries.definition: Hot briquetted iron

entries.name: IF
entries.definition: Induction furnace

entries.name: mtpa
entries.definition: million tonnes per annum

entries.name: OHF
entries.definition: Open hearth furnace

entries.name: ttpa
entries.definition: thousand tonnes per annum

entries.name: Capacity
entries.definition: Capacity is the tonnes of crude iron per annum that an iron unit is capable of producing.

entries.name: Production
entries.definition: Production is only captured at the plant-level. See the "Plant-level data" document.

entries.name: Note
entries.definition: Additional information about the research method and estimations of capacity and production are available on the GEM website at https://globalenergymonitor.org/projects/global-iron-and-steel-tracker/methodology/

entries.name: Announced
entries.definition: Capacity that has been announced in corporate or governmental planning documents, but has not begun construction.

entries.name: Construction
entries.definition: Physical furnace structure building has begun.

entries.name: Operating
entries.definition: Furnace(s) is currently operating and producing iron or steel.

entries.name: Operating pre-retirement
entries.definition: Company has announced plans to retire this iron or steelmaking capacity but it has not yet ceased operations.

entries.name: Mothballed
entries.definition: Iron or steelmaking has been idled such that it cannot be brought into operation immediately, but is not retired.

entries.name: Mothballed pre-retirement
entries.definition: Iron or steelmaking has been idled such that it cannot be brought into operation immediately. It has not yet been permanently retired/decommissioned, but the plant has announced plans to do so in the future.

entries.name: Retired
entries.definition: Furnace has ceased operations and no longer has ability to produce iron or steel. If the capacity has been mothballed for over 5 years it is considered retired.

entries.name: Cancelled
entries.definition: Capacity previously planned or under development that has been cancelled. If no progress or announcements for announced capacity are made after 5 years it is considered to be cancelled.

entries.name: GEM plant ID
entries.definition: A unique GEM identifier is assigned to each plant. Uses format P10000012XXXX where P stands for "plant", and XXXX increases with every new plant added to the GIST.

entries.name: GEM unit ID
entries.definition: A unique GEM identifier is assigned to each blast furnace unit. Uses format U10000014XXXX where U stands for "unit", and XXXX increases with every new unit added to the tracker.

entries.name: Unit name
entries.definition: If the furnace has a unit name (may be a letter or number) assigned to it. "Unknown" indicates that the unit has no known name and the number has been assigned by GEM.

entries.name: GEM wiki page
entries.definition: Link to GEM wiki page.

entries.name: Country/area
entries.definition: Country or territory.

entries.name: Unit status
entries.definition: Operating status of the blast furnace. Categories are "announced", "construction", "operating", "operating pre-retirement", "mothballed", "mothballed pre-retirement", "cancelled", and "retired".

entries.name: Announced date
entries.definition: Date of announcement/proposal of furnace in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Construction date
entries.definition: Date that construction of the furnace began in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Start date
entries.definition: Furnace start date in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Unit age
entries.definition: Furnace age in years.

entries.name: Pre-retirement announcement date
entries.definition: Date the company announced its plans to retire the blast furnace in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Idled date
entries.definition: Date that blast furnace stops iron production, but has not been disassembled or permanently closed, in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Retired date
entries.definition: Date that blast furnace was permanently closed, either after being disassembled or after a period of inactivity (after 5 years of “mothballed”), in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Furnace manufacturer
entries.definition: The company that manufactured the unit, if known.

entries.name: Furnace model
entries.definition: The specific model of the unit, if known.

entries.name: Current capacity (ttpa)
entries.definition: Production capacity of unit in thousands of metric tonnes per annum.

entries.name: Current size (m3)
entries.definition: Physical furnace size (example: 3000 m3).

entries.name: CCS/CCUS
entries.definition: Carbon capture and storage or carbon capture, utilization, and storage. Notes on any carbon capture projects related to the unit.

entries.name: Most recent relining
entries.definition: Most recent relining date in format YYYY-MM-DD where unit has a known relining. "Unknown" indicates the unit has no known relinings or only has relining events with no known dates. This date is the stop date of the relining or the start date if stop date is unknown. Further detail on relinings can be found in the "Blast furnace relinings" sheet.

entries.name: GEM plant ID
entries.definition: A unique GEM identifier is assigned to each plant. Uses format P10000012XXXX where P stands for "plant", and XXXX increases with every new plant added to the GIST.

entries.name: GEM unit ID
entries.definition: A unique GEM identifier is assigned to each direct reduced iron unit. Uses format U10000014XXXX where U stands for "unit", and XXXX increases with every new unit added to the tracker. "N/A" indicates that the number of units at the plant is unknown, so no unit ID has been assigned.

entries.name: Unit name
entries.definition: If the furnace has a unit name (may be a letter or number) assigned to it. "Unknown" indicates that the unit has no known name and the number has been assigned by GEM.

entries.name: GEM wiki page
entries.definition: Link to GEM wiki page.

entries.name: Country/area
entries.definition: Country or territory.

entries.name: Unit status
entries.definition: Operating status of the direct reduced iron unit. Categories are "announced", "construction", "operating", "operating pre-retirement", "mothballed", "mothballed pre-retirement", "cancelled", and "retired".

entries.name: Announced date
entries.definition: Date of announcement/proposal of furnace in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Construction date
entries.definition: Date that construction of the furnace began in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Start date
entries.definition: Furnace start date in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Unit age
entries.definition: Furnace age in years.

entries.name: Pre-retirement announcement date
entries.definition: Date the company announced its plans to retire the direct reduced iron unit in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Idled date
entries.definition: Date that direct reduced iron unit stops iron production, but has not been disassembled or permanently closed, in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Retired date
entries.definition: Date that direct reduced iron unit was permanently closed, either after being disassembled or after a period of inactivity (after 5 years of “mothballed”), in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Furnace manufacturer
entries.definition: The company that manufactured the unit, if known.

entries.name: Furnace model
entries.definition: The specific model of the unit, if known.

entries.name: Furnace type
entries.definition: Options are: "shaft furnace", "fluidized bed", "rotary kiln", "hearth", and "unknown".

entries.name: Current capacity (ttpa)
entries.definition: Production capacity of unit in thousands of metric tonnes per annum.

entries.name: Furnace height
entries.definition: The height of the furnace in meters.

entries.name: Furnace diameter
entries.definition: The diameter or width of the furnace in meters.

entries.name: CCS/CCUS
entries.definition: Notes on any carbon capture projects related to the unit.

entries.name: Current or initial Reductant
entries.definition: Options are: "gas", "coal (solid)", "biomass (solid)", and "unknown".

entries.name: Gas reductant
entries.definition: If "gas" is selected as reductant. Options are: "syngas (reformed methane)", "syngas (gasified coal)", "syngas (biomass)", "fossil gas (unreformed)", "waste gas recovery (coke oven)", "waste gas recovery (other)", ""hydrogen", and "gas (unknown type)".

entries.name: Current or initial hydrogen reductant color
entries.definition: If "hydrogen" is selected as gas reductant type. If the furnace uses or plans to use hydrogen as a reductant upon startup, this is the color of that hydrogen. Options are: "green", "blue", "gray", "black", "pink", "turquoise", "yellow", "white", and "unknown".

entries.name: Hydrogen reductant status
entries.definition: Notes whether the furnace is currently capable of using hydrogen as a reductant.

entries.name: Future hydrogen reductant color
entries.definition: If the furnace plans to switch to hydrogen as a reductant in the future, or plans to switch hydrogen colors in the future, this is the color of that hydrogen. Options are: "green", "blue", "gray", "black", "pink", "turquoise", "yellow", "white", and "unknown".

entries.name: Hydrogen reductant conversion date
entries.definition: Date when the furnace is set to switch to hydrogen reductant.

entries.name: GEM unit ID
entries.definition: A unique GEM identifier is assigned to each blast furnace unit. Uses format U10000014XXXX where U stands for "unit", and XXXX increases with every new unit added to the tracker.

entries.name: Relining status
entries.definition: Categories are "complete", "in progress", "planned", or "cancelled".

entries.name: Relining type
entries.definition: Type of relining performed on blast furnace unit. Categories are "full", "partial" and "unknown".

entries.name: Furnace relining start date
entries.definition: Date that furnace stops operations and transitions to maintenance/relining work in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Furnace relining stop date
entries.definition: Date that furnace resumes operations and begins transition back to ironmaking operations in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

entries.name: Furnace relining duration (days)
entries.definition: Approximate number of days that blast furnace unit was stopped to undergo maintenance.

entries.name: Furnace relining number
entries.definition: Number of the furnace relining if known.

entries.name: Relining cost
entries.definition: Approximate investment cost of equipment, installation, upgrades, technology, etc for relining work. Does not include revenue lost during relining of blast furnace. Only numerical digits with currency type found in the Relining Cost Unit field.

entries.name: Relining cost unit
entries.definition: ISO three-letter currency code unit for relining cost.

entries.name: Relining capacity expansion
entries.definition: If applicable, furnace expansion as a result of the relining maintenance work. "N/A" if known that relining will not result in capacity expansion. "unknown" if not known whether relining will result in capacity expansion.

entries.name: Relining capacity expansion unit
entries.definition: Typically cubic meters (m3) for blast furnace volume.
